channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power was restored to more than 800,000 residents in Kyiv on Saturday following major Russian attacks on Ukraine’s power grid that wounded at least 20 people and caused widespread blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, The Hill

item.title: Russian strikes wound at least 20 in Ukraine's capital as child is killed in separate attack
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drone and missile strikes wounded at least 20 people in Kyiv, damaged residential buildings and caused blackouts across swaths of Ukraine early Friday, authorities said. A child also was killed in separate attacks in the southeast of the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Republican leaders backed Venezuela's Machado for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., signed a letter supporting opposition leader María Corina Machado.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens countermeasures after China tightens grip on rare earths, says 'no reason' to meet Xi
item.description: President Trump on Friday threatened an increase in tariffs on China and other potential countermeasures after Beijing tightened control over rare earth elements and critical mineral exports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel-Hamas ceasefire takes effect, IDF begins pulling back in Gaza
item.description: Israeli troops began repositioning to pre-determined locations as the ceasefire with Hamas began, bringing the hostages one step closer to going home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Burkina Faso rejects proposal to accept deportees from the US
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Burkina Faso says it has refused a proposal from the Trump administration to accept deportees from the United States. The West African country was asked whether it would accept non-citizens expelled by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World leaders express hope after Israel and Hamas agreed to first phase of plan to end Gaza war
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — World leaders on Thursday expressed hope for peace and urged Israel and Hamas to fulfill their commitments in the hours after U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's new missiles and drones cause gas shortages in Russia, Zelenskyy says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Strikes on Russian oil facilities by Ukraine’s newly developed long-range missiles and drones are causing significant gas shortages in Russia, while on the battlefield a recent Ukrainian counter-offensive has derailed Russia’s plans to capture parts of the eastern Donetsk region
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US diplomat fired over relationship with woman accused of Chinese Communist Party ties
item.description: The dismissal is believed to be the first of its kind for violating a ban on such relationships that was introduced late last year under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Celebrations in Israel, Gaza at first peace deal announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump says the remaining Israeli hostages in Gaza may be released as soon as Monday.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree to part of Trump's Gaza peace plan, will free hostages and prisoners
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israel and Hamas agreed Wednesday to pause fighting in Gaza so that the remaining hostages there can be freed in the coming days in exchange for Palestinian prisoners, accepting elements of a plan put forward by the Trump administration that would represent the biggest breakthrough
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, SAM MEDNICK and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas accepts Trump peace plan ending 2 years of war in Gaza, returning hostages
item.description: Hamas agrees to President Donald Trump's Gaza peace plan that calls for release of all 48 hostages and complete Israeli withdrawal in exchange for Hamas disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Before and after images show devastating destruction in Gaza
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals widespread devastation in Gaza cities, including Rafah and Beit Hanoun, since the start of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your phone rings, and it's a number from Sweden. Do you answer? A Nobel Prize winner didn't
item.description: This year’s Nobel Prize science winners got the good news from early morning phone calls and door knocks.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio And Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian strike seriously damages Ukrainian power plant as winter approaches, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia seriously damaged one of Ukraine’s thermal power plants in an overnight attack, authorities said Wednesday, as Moscow pursued its campaign to deny Ukrainians heat, light and running water as winter approaches.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli military intercepts another flotilla heading to Gaza and detains scores of activists
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military intercepted a nine-boat flotilla trying to break Israel’s naval blockade of Gaza early Wednesday in the Mediterranean Sea and detained scores of activists on board, the flotilla organizers and the Israeli Foreign Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel seizes another Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel intercepted another flotilla attempting to break the naval blockade and reach Gaza. This comes just days after another fleet was intercepted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Trump’s Gaza deal nears, family warns Israel not to free another Sinwar
item.description: As President Trump pushes to finalize a Gaza ceasefire and hostage exchange, one Israeli family warns that freeing a notorious Hamas terrorist could lead to new bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deadline for Mexico to pay water owed to US fast approaching
item.description: The clock is ticking down on Mexico's deadline this month to pay the United States water it owes under a 1944 international treaty.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza peace talks appear to gain momentum on the war's anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Peace talks between Israel and Hamas resumed at an Egyptian resort city on Tuesday, the two-year anniversary of the militant group's surprise attack on Israel that started the war that has killed tens of thousands of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu, on 2-year mark of Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack, says Israel 'not broken,' vows to bring hostages home
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu marked two years since Hamas’ Oct. 7 assault in a statement issued on X, honoring the victims and vowing Israel’s war for survival continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump thanks group representing families of hostages for Nobel Peace Prize appeal
item.description: President Donald Trump thanked the Hostages and Missing Families Forum for its appeal to the Nobel committee to award the president the Nobel Peace Prize.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuban-led caravan aims for Mexico City as Trump policies deter migrants from US
item.description: Cuban migrants in Mexico choose Mexico City over U.S. destinations as President Donald Trump's strict immigration policies influence regional migration trends.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newly elected German mayor fights for life after stabbing attack
item.description: German mayor-elect Iris Stalzer was hospitalized in critical condition following a stabbing attack at her home in the German town of Herdecke.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare ancient artwork vanishes from tomb in Egypt: Report
item.description: The artwork, a 4,000-year-old limestone relief, was reported to have disappeared over the weekend from a Fifth Dynasty tomb.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Big Time Rush's James Maslow releases new song celebrating Israel
item.description: Maslow acknowledged he chose Oct. 7th because of how the "country (Israel) and this world have normalized a hate towards each other.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Jilly Cooper, British author of bestselling risqué novels, including 'Rivals,' dies at 88
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Jilly Cooper, the bestselling British author known for her chronicles of class and sex in risqué novels, including “Rivals” and “Riders,” has died unexpectedly, her agent and family said Monday. She was 88.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Greta Thunberg is among flotilla activists deported from Israel. Others remain in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli authorities said Monday they deported to Greece and Slovakia another 171 people detained for taking part in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla, including prominent Swedish activist Greta Thunberg.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Negotiators meet in Egypt seeking Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Talks are split into phases: first, securing the release of hostages, then tackling Hamas' demilitarization.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: French Prime Minister resigns after less than a month in office
item.description: Facing criticism from all sides, France’s new prime minister Sébastien Lecornu resigned less than 24 hours after naming his government
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says US hit another boat off Venezuela coast on Saturday
item.description: It was not immediately clear if the president was referencing a strike announced Friday by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK police given more time to question 4 suspects over deadly Manchester synagogue attack
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. police were given extra time Saturday to question four people arrested on suspicion of terror offenses after an attack on a synagogue in northwest England that left two people dead and Britain’s Jewish community shocked and grieving.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
